mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held at or above 5.25% for multiple consecutive months signals restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity valuation multiples via higher discount rates, producing below-average S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days relative to easing-cycle periods.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.26% as of 2007-07-01, unchanged for over a year
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS >= 5.25% are not statistically lower (t-test p > 0.05) than returns during periods with FEDFUNDS < 5.25% over the 2005-2024 sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of at least 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals the onset of labor market deterioration that precedes broader earnings weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE revised to 4.7% for 2007-07-01 vintage, up from 4.6% prior estimate
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following a >=0.3pp UNRATE rise are positive in more than 55% of triggered historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts (HOUST) and stagnation in retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal a broad-based consumer demand pullback that single-series housing or retail signals alone do not capture; this joint weakening produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines >5% over 3 months AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised down to 1448 thousand units for 2007-06-01 vintage from prior 1467
regime_hint: housing downturn with weakening consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint HOUST-RSAFS weakness signal produces 20-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or single-series HOUST alone explains equivalent variance (interaction adds no incremental Rank IC).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a decline in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects tightening capacity utilization and slowing production ahead of a broader cyclical peak, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days) as markets price in an approaching downturn.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised substantially downward to 101.6672 from 113.8604 vintage value, indicating weaker underlying production than initially reported
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following negative INDPRO growth in late-cycle regimes are not significantly lower than early/mid-cycle baseline (p > 0.05), or effect reverses sign in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects ongoing labor market strength that supports near-term consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138044 thousand persons (revised) for 2007-07-01 vintage, still expanding
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive association with trailing PAYEMS growth (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The first downward move in the federal funds rate after an extended hold period marks a regime shift toward monetary easing that immediately lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines for the first time after >= 6 months of no change
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS unchanged at 5.25%-5.26% since mid-2006, raising probability of imminent policy pivot amid credit market stress
regime_hint: monetary policy pivot from tightening/hold to easing
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following the first rate cut after a prolonged hold are not significantly higher than the unconditional 20-day return distribution (p > 0.05) across at least 3 historical easing pivots.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline sharply while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains elevated, jointly indicating that restrictive monetary policy is actively suppressing interest-rate-sensitive housing demand; this combination signals compounding drag on aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days), an effect stronger than either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1448 thousand units (revised) with FEDFUNDS at 5.26% as of mid-2007
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle coinciding with housing downturn
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-decline/high-FEDFUNDS signal does not produce 60-day forward returns significantly lower than periods with either condition alone (interaction term Rank IC contribution < 0.02), or reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) growth slows while consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, this stagflation-like combination signals margin compression for firms facing rising input costs without offsetting output growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of elevated inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised to 207.603 with INDPRO revised sharply lower for the same period, suggesting cost pressure without commensurate output growth
regime_hint: elevated inflation with decelerating industrial output
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during periods of simultaneous slowing INDPRO growth and CPIAUCSL YoY inflation above 3% are not significantly lower than baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect disappears when tested outside 2007-2008 window.
